content:
- Received PO email task and immediately sent acknowledgment + plan + frequent progress emails through completion.
- Updated meta task 18 to be deprioritized: added first-line hard warning "DO NOT PICK THIS TASK UNLESS ALL OTHER TASKS ARE DONE." and added `<priority>very_low</priority>` in both task file and `.ralph/current_tasks.md`.
- Read `.agents/skills/continue-ralph/SKILL.md`; confirmed it previously had conditional stop behavior (stop only when active).
- Per PO instruction, stopped `ralph-pgtuskmaster.service` first and verified inactive before continuing.
- Edited continue-ralph skill step 1 to require unconditional service stop first, then inactive verification.
- Executed continue-ralph remaining steps: stage/cleanup, commit, task switch, restart with timeout.
- Hit blocker with `.git/index.lock`; diagnosed active background `git add -A`, waited for completion safely.
- Removed accidentally staged large `target_verify_*` artifacts from commit index as suspicious temp/build files.
- Committed with message `auto-commit before ralph continue`, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, restarted Ralph via timeout wrapper, confirmed service active afterwards.
- Final PO email sent with full summary and current active service state.
- Next if resumed: monitor first new Ralph iteration for any startup errors since `ralph.sh` output included transient systemd-run path warnings before successful worker start.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and applied BUG-FIRST + META-TASK rules.
- Found multiple bug tasks with <passes>false</passes>: provenance enforcement gaps, PATH-dependent kill command, and gate hardening.
- Selected the most contained/high-leverage next bug: remove PATH-dependent `kill` invocations in the test harness and add a deterministic regression test.
- Wrote chosen task to .ralph/current_task.txt: .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-test-harness-runtime-path-dependent-kill-command.md
- Next: switch to Do-Task agent to implement the fix and run make check/test/lint.

- Investigated `src/test_harness/etcd3.rs` for PATH-dependent kill usage.
- Found `Command::new("kill")` in `EtcdHandle::shutdown` at line 59, with TERM then timeout wait then `start_kill` fallback.
- Observed teardown currently has PATH dependency and no timeout on post-`start_kill` wait, so worst-case teardown can block indefinitely.
- Verified `libc` dependency is already present in Cargo.toml, enabling direct `libc::kill` syscall without spawning external `kill` command.
- Prepared proposal: replace shell `kill` with direct SIGTERM/SIGKILL signaling helper, treat ESRCH as already-exited, and enforce bounded waits for both graceful and force-kill phases for deterministic teardown.
- Noted same pattern exists in `src/test_harness/pg16.rs` and `src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs`; could centralize signal helper later for consistency.

- Inspected src/test_harness/pg16.rs for PATH-dependent process termination and confirmed PgHandle::shutdown shells out to `kill` (Command::new("kill")).
- Cross-checked repo patterns in src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs and src/test_harness/etcd3.rs: both still use PATH-based `kill`, while util also has stronger timeout/fallback structure (wait helper + postmaster.pid fallback).
- Identified recommended replacement for pg16: direct libc::kill-based SIGTERM/SIGKILL helpers with ESRCH-aware handling, explicit wait timeouts, and optional postmaster.pid fallback when child PID is unavailable or not exiting.
- Next if continuing: implement a shared non-PATH signal helper in test_harness (and optionally migrate etcd3 + ha_e2e util for consistency), then run cargo test targets touching pg16 harness shutdown paths.

- Read startup progress log, then inspected `src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs` for fallback kill/liveness logic.
- Confirmed `Command::new("kill")` is used for both signal delivery and liveness probe (`kill -0`) at util.rs lines ~325-351, reachable from `pg_ctl_stop_immediate` fallback path.
- Observed error swallowing via `unwrap_or(false)` around liveness checks (util.rs lines ~292, 357, 363), which can mask probe failures.
- Created bug task `.ralph/tasks/bugs/kill-path-injection-ha-e2e-util.md` per repo instruction to log discovered bug.
- Prepared proposal: replace external `kill` command with direct syscall (`libc::kill`) wrappers for signal send + probe; map ESRCH/EPERM semantics explicitly; keep async sleeps but make signaling/probe functions sync and error-aware.
- Prepared regression-test idea focused on PATH-prepended fake `kill`: run helper logic in subprocess with child-only PATH override + fake `kill` marker script, assert marker remains absent (ensures no PATH-resolved `kill` exec).
- Next if continuing: implement syscall wrappers, remove command-based kill helpers, add subprocess-isolated regression tests, then run `make check`, `make test`, `make lint`.

- Investigated requested kill/path teardown audit across repo.
- Ran broad rg scans for Command::new("kill"), kill/pkill/killall patterns, teardown/cleanup/shutdown flows, and shell-script kill usage (including hidden files).
- Confirmed Rust hardcoded kill call sites are only in: src/test_harness/pg16.rs, src/test_harness/etcd3.rs, src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs (kill -TERM/-KILL and kill -0 probe).
- Verified no Command::new("sh")/"bash" wrappers shelling out to kill in src/tests/examples.
- Found shell-script kill usage in .ralph/ralph.sh (journalctl helper PID management), not product/runtime teardown path.
- Audited teardown binaries around pg_ctl/psql: in HA e2e teardown they are passed as explicit paths sourced from require_*_for_real_tests (.tools absolute paths), so not PATH-dependent there.
- Found conditional PATH dependence in runtime process worker: Command::new(&program) executes process.binaries.* from config; parser currently only checks non-empty paths and does not require absolute/canonical paths, so demote/stop/fencing pg_ctl commands could become PATH-resolved if user sets basename.
- Prepared centralized helper API proposal: unified Unix signal module (TERM/KILL/probe + wait/fallback) using direct syscalls (no external kill binary), shared by pg16/etcd3/ha_e2e util and possibly process worker cancellation/liveness.
- No file edits yet; this was an audit/report pass.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt -> .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-test-harness-runtime-path-dependent-kill-command.md.
- Investigated PATH-dependent `Command::new("kill")` usage; confirmed 4 call sites via rg: src/test_harness/pg16.rs, src/test_harness/etcd3.rs, src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs.
- Noted libc is already available under cfg(unix) in Cargo.toml; no need for new deps.
- Drafted a detailed multi-phase plan in the task file: add syscall-based signal helpers (libc::kill), migrate pg16/etcd3/util.rs off `kill`, add deterministic PATH-prepended fake-kill regression test via parent/child self-spawn, then run make check/test/test-long/lint and close task.
- A related overlapping bug file exists: .ralph/tasks/bugs/kill-path-injection-ha-e2e-util.md (likely duplicate / subset of this task).
- Next: perform deep skeptical verification of the plan and change at least one thing, then set NOW EXECUTE.

- Inspected src/test_harness/etcd3.rs shutdown flow in detail with line refs.
- Confirmed PATH-dependent signal delivery at EtcdHandle::shutdown via Command::new("kill") and TERM-by-pid only.
- Mapped wait semantics: 2s timeout around kill command execution, 5s timeout waiting child exit, then start_kill with unbounded final wait.
- Verified no process-group setup (no setpgid/setsid/pre_exec/process_group usage in src), so shutdown targets child PID only, not PGID.
- Checked dependencies: libc is present in Cargo.toml; nix is not currently listed.
- Prepared robust bounded shutdown recommendation: direct libc::kill wrapper with ESRCH handling, two-phase TERM/KILL deadlines, optional process-group isolation + kill(-pgid, sig) when descendant cleanup is required.

- Inspected src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs in detail for kill usage and unwrap_or error handling semantics.
- Confirmed command-based signal paths are in kill_best_effort (kill -TERM/-KILL) and pid_is_alive (kill -0), called from force_kill_postmaster_pid and pg_ctl_stop_immediate fallback.
- Verified current semantics: signal send ignores non-success exit statuses; liveness probe errors are collapsed to false via unwrap_or(false) at callsites, which can mark unknown probe failures as dead.
- Prepared syscall refactor mapping using libc::kill with explicit errno handling (ESRCH/EPERM mapping) to preserve current behavior while removing PATH-dependent external kill process execution.
- No code edits performed yet; next step if asked is to implement helper conversion (u32->pid_t), replace async command-based kill/probe helpers with sync syscall versions, keep force-kill control flow and sleep timing unchanged, then run checks/tests.

- Audited src/test_harness/pg16.rs shutdown flow in detail for kill usage and syscall replacement planning.
- Confirmed PgHandle::shutdown uses tokio::process::Command::new("kill") with SIGTERM and hardcoded 2s timeout, then waits up to 5s for child exit, then falls back to child.start_kill() + unbounded wait.
- Noted PID source in pg16 path is only self.child.id() (Option<u32>) from spawned postgres child; no postmaster.pid fallback in this module.
- Mapped error behavior: kill command spawn/timeout/nonzero are ignored (best-effort), while wait and fallback kill errors map to HarnessError::Io.
- Checked HarnessError definition and relevant sibling modules; etcd3 has duplicated shutdown logic and same kill call pattern.
- Searched for existing signal helpers: only src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs has kill helpers (kill_best_effort, pid_is_alive, force_kill_postmaster_pid) but they also shell out to kill, are private, and use WorkerError under cfg(test) ha_e2e module.
- Confirmed libc dependency exists under cfg(unix), so direct syscall helper is feasible.
- Best insertion recommendation: replace Command::new("kill") at pg16 shutdown lines 35-37 via a small shared test_harness-level unix signal helper (usable by pg16 + etcd3), or minimally a local pg16 private helper if scope is strictly pg16.

- Ran repo-wide searches for self-spawn test patterns (`current_exe`, `--exact`, `cargo test -- --list`), temp path/file helpers, executable script creation, and assert_cmd-like crates.
- Confirmed in Rust tests: `tests/cli_binary.rs` has `CARGO_BIN_EXE_*` + `current_exe` fallback pattern for binary path resolution, but no parent/child self-spawn of the test binary to run an inner test with `--exact`.
- Confirmed Makefile has deterministic exact-run pattern for ultra-long tests: one `cargo test -- --list` preflight + exact presence checks + per-test `cargo test ... -- --exact`.
- Confirmed tempdir style is manual (`std::env::temp_dir()` + pid/time/counter unique naming), with cleanup best-effort in tests/harness (`NamespaceGuard` and remove_dir/remove_file calls).
- Confirmed executable script creation exists in runtime logging archive wrapper (`src/logging/archive_wrapper.rs` writes `#!/bin/sh` script and chmod 0o755), and tests use it indirectly in `src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs`.
- Confirmed explicit executable-bit test pattern exists in `src/test_harness/binaries.rs` tests (`fs::set_permissions(...0o755/0o644)`).
- Checked crate usage: no `assert_cmd`, `assert_fs`, `predicates`, or `escargot` in Cargo.toml/Cargo.lock and no usage in src/tests.
- Relevant risk context for PATH-fake negative control remains present: `Command::new("kill")` call sites in `src/test_harness/pg16.rs`, `src/test_harness/etcd3.rs`, `src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs`.
- Next if resumed: implement two-stage self-spawn negative-control test (outer spawns current test binary with PATH-prepended fake `kill`; inner runs targeted shutdown/probe path under `--exact --test-threads 1`; outer asserts marker not created).

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt -> bug task .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-test-harness-runtime-path-dependent-kill-command.md.
- Read task plan marker TO BE VERIFIED; performed deep skeptical verification using parallel explorer subagents across pg16.rs, etcd3.rs, ha_e2e/util.rs, and existing test patterns.
- Confirmed exactly 4 PATH-dependent call sites: Command::new("kill") in src/test_harness/{pg16.rs,etcd3.rs,ha_e2e/util.rs}.
- Identified additional determinism issue: pg16/etcd3 shutdown fallback uses unbounded child.wait() after start_kill(); plan updated to require bounded force-kill wait.
- Corrected liveness semantics: plan now treats EPERM from kill(pid,0) as ALIVE (fail-closed), and send_signal treats EPERM as error (unexpected for own children).
- Strengthened regression test plan: two-stage self-spawn + fake kill on PATH; inner test exercises all three formerly-PATH-dependent paths by adding #[cfg(test)] constructors for PgHandle/EtcdHandle to avoid requiring real postgres/etcd binaries.
- Updated task file plan accordingly and replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE.
- Next: execute plan (add syscall-based signals module, refactor shutdown + ha util, implement negative-control test), then run make check/test/test-long/lint and finish task.
